response:
Sub-problem 1:  
The number of rooms requiring attention follows a Poisson distribution with a mean of 20. The probability that exactly 15 rooms need attention is calculated using the Poisson probability formula:  
P(x) = (e^-λ * λ^x) / x!  
Where λ = 20 and x = 15  
P(15) = (e^-20 * 20^15) / 15!  
The value of e^-20 is approximately 2.0611536224385575e-09  
20^15 is 32768000000000000  
15! is 1307674368000  
P(15) = (2.0611536224385575e-09 * 32768000000000000) / 1307674368000  
P(15) = 0.0459  

Sub-problem 2:  
The concierge assists with 8 concerns and has a success rate of 90%. This is a binomial distribution with n = 8 and p = 0.9  
The probability that at least 7 of these concerns are resolved successfully is the sum of the probabilities of exactly 7 and exactly 8 successes  
P(x ≥ 7) = P(x = 7) + P(x = 8)  
P(x = 7) = (8 choose 7) * (0.9)^7 * (0.1)^1 = 8 * 0.4782969 * 0.1 = 0.38263752  
P(x = 8) = (8 choose 8) * (0.9)^8 * (0.1)^0 = 1 * 0.43046721 * 1 = 0.43046721  
P(x ≥ 7) = 0.38263752 + 0.43046721 = 0.81310473
